title: Mas Que Nada
source: freemidi.org (Sergio Mendes feat. Black Eyed Peas 2006 arrangement of the Jorge Ben Jor song, 111 measures, 4:22; replaced the 1,905-byte 21-measure bitmidi uploads/72364 fragment — a mislabeled Mario file — 2026-07-09 data-audit follow-up)

musicalLanguage.description: Jorge Ben Jor's Mas Que Nada appears as a 111-measure samba transcription at 101 BPM carrying 14,306 notes — full-band density rendered onto two staves, peaking at 39 onsets in measures 43, 63, 71, 103, and 107. Like Afro-Cuban clave-based music, samba organizes everything around one repeating rhythmic cell, and the data shows exactly that: a 32-note right-hand figure recurs 34 times (compression ratio 16.485), covering 62% of the right hand's onsets, locked against a 26-note left-hand cell recurring 38 times (compression 15.438, 59% of the left hand's onsets). Both cells span two measures — the groove's true length. Harmony stays almost entirely below the confidence gate: exactly one window in 111 measures, an implied C# major at measure 4, so no progression story is claimable here; the engine is rhythm. The right hand touches G#6 at measure 43; the left hand spans C2 (measure 7) up to B3 (measure 23).
musicalLanguage.structure: Nine regions, but the story is one boundary: the groove cell enters at measure 7 with novelty 7.049 — far above every later pivot in the piece (none exceeds 0.747) — and then the cell simply never leaves. The right-hand form recurs at 34 two-measure spans from measures 7-8 to 101-102; the left-hand form at 38 spans over the same territory. Above the engine, a 78-note right-hand phrase recurs at measures 42-46, 62-66, 70-74, 102-106, and 106-110, and a 30-note left-hand fill punctuates at nine spots (measures 24-25, 44-45, 62-63, 64-65, 70-71, 72-73, 102-103, 104-105, and 108-109). The frame is near-empty: measure 1 is silent, measure 2 carries 2 onsets, and measure 111 closes on a single onset.
keyMoments: The two-measure groove cell enters at measure 7 with the file's overwhelming structural event (novelty 7.049, with no later boundary above 0.747) and never leaves: 34 right-hand and 38 left-hand recurrences carry roughly six tenths of each hand's onsets.
A longer 78-note right-hand phrase recurs at measures 42-46, 62-66, 70-74, 102-106, and 106-110 — the section-scale hook riding on top of the two-measure engine.

Exactly one chord window clears the confidence gate in the entire piece — an implied C# major at measure 4 — and the analysis is candid that this reflects the gate holding back dense, blended voicings, not evidence of harmonic emptiness.
The registral ceiling and a density peak share a bar: the right hand touches G#6 at measure 43, one of the five 39-onset measures (43, 63, 71, 103, 107).
teachingGoals: Master the two-measure cell before anything else: practice the right-hand figure of measures 7-8 and the left-hand figure of the same bars separately, then lock them together — 34 and 38 recurrences make this one coordination most of the piece.
Learn the 78-note phrase at measures 42-46 as the second layer, then recognize its returns at 62-66, 70-74, 102-106, and 106-110 so each arrival is recall rather than sight-reading.
Practice holding the cell steady at full density — the 39-onset peaks at measures 43, 63, 71, 103, and 107 all arrive while the groove is running, so stamina inside the pattern is the real skill.
Hear the piece as rhythm-first: with one implied label in 111 measures, listen for how the cell's accent pattern creates the sense of harmonic motion rather than the reverse.
styleTips: Feel the two-measure cycle as the unit of time — like clave-organized music, samba locks every voice to one repeating cell; listen for which of the two measures carries the heavier accents and keep that weighting identical on every pass.

Play the left-hand cell with percussive, staccato attacks — it is the surdo-and-bass layer of a samba band on piano; listen for its low C2 (measure 7) landing like a drum: dry, short, exactly in time.
Don't let the 39-onset peak measures (43, 63, 71, 103, 107) get louder just because they are busier — hold the dynamic ceiling and let density itself supply the intensity.

Use the near-empty frame to reset: measure 1 is silent and measure 111 carries a single onset — begin from stillness, and cut the final onset cleanly rather than inventing a fadeout the file doesn't write.
